backup.auto_browser_hint: Semi-automatic: requires the management center to be open in a browser tab — auto backup stops when the browser is closed
backup.auto_note_1: Auto backup runs in your browser, independent of the backend. Each time you open the management center (any page), if the "Min Interval" has elapsed since the last backup, a backup runs immediately. While the center stays open, backups continue at the set interval.
backup.auto_note_2: Closing the browser tab or leaving the management center stops auto backup — it resumes automatically next time you open it.
backup.auto_note_3: "Max Backups" controls how many backup files are retained on WebDAV; oldest files are deleted when the limit is exceeded.
backup.auto_subtitle: Automatically back up data to WebDAV when enabled; old backups are cleaned up when the limit is exceeded
backup.config_warning_message: The backend config (config.yaml) contains sensitive information such as API keys. Although backup files are encoded, this is not strong encryption. Please ensure your WebDAV server is secure and keep backup files safe.
backup.delete_confirm_message: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
backup.error_no_connection: Please configure and save WebDAV connection first
backup.manual_subtitle: All backup data (including config and keys) is encoded and not human-readable, but this is not strong encryption and cannot prevent professional decryption. All WebDAV traffic is relayed through the backend server — your WebDAV credentials never appear in browser network requests. We recommend connecting to your WebDAV server over HTTPS and keeping backup files secure.
backup.page_subtitle: Runtime data like usage stats is lost on backend restart — back up regularly via WebDAV for data persistence and one-click restore
backup.restore_card_subtitle: Restore data from WebDAV server or local backup file to the current management center
backup.restore_local_hint: Select a previously exported .json backup file to restore
backup.restore_success: Restore successful. Some settings may require a page refresh to take effect
backup.scope_config_detail: Full content of backend config.yaml, including API keys, proxy settings, routing strategy and other sensitive info
backup.scope_config_restore_hint: View only, will not write to backend automatically
backup.scope_localStorage_detail: Theme, language, sidebar state, auth files page size, model prices and other browser local storage
backup.scope_usage_detail: Export request counts, token usage, success/failure stats from backend (lost on backend restart)

payload.default_raw_rules_desc: When parameters are missing, write these values as raw JSON fragments such as true, 123, "high", or {"type":"object"}
payload.default_rules_desc: Use these default values when parameters are not specified in the request
payload.description: Default values, raw JSON rules, override rules, and filter rules
payload.filter_rules_desc: Pre-filter upstream request body via JSON Path, automatically remove non-compliant/redundant parameters (Request Sanitization)
payload.override_raw_rules_desc: Always overwrite parameter values as raw JSON fragments, useful for response_format, schemas, and other complex fields
payload.override_rules_desc: Force override parameter values in the request
